title: Scope Resolution Operator (::) Usage
description: A visual guide demonstrating the primary uses of the scope resolution operator in C++ to access names from different scopes.
relationships: The :: operator resolves name conflicts by specifying the scope (global, class, or namespace) to look in
It is used to define member functions outside their class body
It accesses static members of a class without an object instance
It is used to access names hidden by local variables with the same name
